decision_summary: Approve Agent Session Observability as the strongest post-v6.9 direction candidate.
rationale: The external Copilot session streaming signal shows that agent prompts, responses, and tool calls are becoming an enterprise observability surface. AOF should absorb the vendor-neutral pattern by treating runtime session events as canonical evidence, while keeping the first implementation local and file-backed.
recommendation: Use v7.0 direction review to evaluate a bounded local runtime event stream with session-event records, stream audit, artifact-write provenance, tool-call safety metadata, loop stop events, and Mission Control projection.
target_audience: AOF operator choosing the next frontier after v6.9.0.
expected_user_reaction: The operator should understand that this direction makes AOF work reconstructable as a live process, not just as final artifacts.
artifact_change_recommendations: Add session-event-record and runtime-event-stream schemas in the next implementation release.
Add session-observability-audit before claiming runtime session observability.
Project event stream state into Mission Control without making the viewer a source of truth.
organization_change_recommendations: Treat prompts, responses, tool calls, artifact writes, decisions, reviews, and stop/defer/reopen outcomes as runtime evidence events.
Keep vendor-specific APIs as optional providers rather than core AOF dependencies.
